content:
# Session 84 (iter-084) ‚Äî review

## Metadata
- Active-cone sorry: **3 ‚Üí 2** (REAL elimination). `TensorObjInverse.lean`
  {`dual_unit_iso_restrict_compat` L1567 (S4a), `trivialisation_restrict_compat` L2335 (telescope)};
  PresheafDualPullback.lean 0; SliceTransport.lean 0.
- SOLO `prove` lane; one `.lean` file edited (`TensorObjInverse.lean`) + MEMORY.md.
- `lake build AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.TensorObjInverse` ‚Üí **EXIT 0 (8325 jobs)** ‚Äî re-verified this
  review (green-mod-sorry, only longLine + 1 deprecation warning). Axiom-clean: `dual_restrict_iso_comp`
  and `dual_restrict_iso_restrict_compat` both `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` (kernel-only,
  no `sorryAx`) ‚Äî independently confirmed by lvb-checker.
- Targets attempted: L1 `dual_restrict_iso_comp` STEP-B (primary ‚Äî SOLVED); S4a (probed, blocked);
  telescope (not attempted, gated on S4a).

## Target 1 ‚Äî `dual_restrict_iso_comp` STEP-B (was L1384) ‚Äî SOLVED (primary)
The inline `‚òÖpb-iso` SCP-cocycle (bridge (c)/STEP-A already applied) closed ‚Üí **S3
`dual_restrict_iso_restrict_compat` now fully sorry-free**. The ~15-iter `restrictFunctor j`
SOURCE-cat instance-diamond cone is fully retired on the dual-restriction leg.

Attempt trail (from `attempts_raw.jsonl`):
1. `rw [hb]` (iso-form bridge (b)) `; simp only [Iso.trans_assoc]` ‚Üí **fail**: `rw` did not find
   pattern though visibly present (defeq-not-syntactic across the `SheafOfModules ‚â´` seam).
2. `erw [hb] ; simp only [Iso.trans_assoc]` ‚Üí **fail**: `erw` fires but leaves an iso-bracket that
   `simp [Iso.trans_assoc]` cannot reassociate ("no `(?a ‚â™‚â´ ?b) ‚â™‚â´ ?c` redex"). Staying iso-level is dead.
3. **WINNER**: `apply Iso.ext` ‚Üí `simp only [Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom, Functor.map_comp,
   Iso.symm_hom, Iso.app_hom]` (syntactic `.hom` flatten ‚Äî `Category.assoc` fires, never unfolds
   `Œ∏_g`'s `sliceDualTransport` internals) ‚Üí `exact dual_scp_assemble ha2 haZc hcancel1 hcancelrd hnat`.

The five concrete `have`s feeding the NEW generic helper `dual_scp_assemble` (single abstract
`[Category C]`, pure `‚â´` bookkeeping ‚Äî head-cancel + mid-cancel + naturality):
- `ha2` = bridge (b) `sheafificationCompPullback_comp f g M.val.dual` (SCP cocycle).
- `haZc` = distribute outer `sheafify` over the bridge-(c) composite via `‚Üê Functor.map_comp √ó3`
  (syntactic, never touches `Œ∏_g`).
- `hcancel1` = `Iso.hom_inv_id_app _ _` (`pullbackComp f g` ‚ãÑ sheaf-`pullbackComp` inverse pair;
  Scheme/Sheaf + `M.dual = aX.obj dM` defeqs handled by `:=`).
- `hcancelrd` = term-mode `(Functor.map_comp _ _ _).symm.trans (congrArg aZ.map (Iso.hom_inv_id_app ‚Ä¶)
  |>.trans (Functor.map_id ‚Ä¶))` ‚Äî mid `PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp` pair under `sheafify`
  (keyed `rw [Iso.hom_inv_id_app]` MISSES across the presheaf-over-Z composite-functor seam ‚Üí `congrArg`).
- `hnat` = `(‚Ä¶sheafificationCompPullback œÜ_f).hom.naturality Œ∏_g.hom |>.symm` (the SCP naturality;
  `.symm` because Mathlib `NatTrans.naturality` orients `F.map ‚â´ app = app ‚â´ G.map`).

**Why `dual_scp_assemble` works where iter-080 monolithic `s3_assemble` whnf-bombed**: objects are
opaque `C` variables, morphisms are variables ‚áí `exact` unifies up to the Scheme/Sheaf-`pullback` +
c.1 pushforward/restrict defeqs WITHOUT triggering the `sheafify`-colimit whnf (the Scheme-typed
`s3_assemble` forced whnf at 6.4M HB). Dual mirror of the proven tensor `pullbackTensorMap_restrict`.

## Target 2 ‚Äî `dual_unit_iso_restrict_compat` (S4a, L1567) ‚Äî BLOCKED (named obstacle)
Probed (`simp only [dual_unit_iso, dual_restrict_iso_eq_comparison]`, already in file). It is a
**separate substantial construction**, NOT a reuse of L1: the residual needs a **new Œ∏-at-unit
bridge** ‚Äî identify `presheafDualPullbackComparison j (unit U)` with `presheafDualUnitIso` modulo
`unitRestrictIso j`. ~100+ LOC, the unit-side analogue of the entire Cone-B Œ∏ machinery. The
`dual_scp_assemble` device does NOT transfer (different shape: `dual_unit_iso = sheafify(presheafDualUnitIso)
‚â™‚â´ counit`, no SCP-composition cocycle). Unit-automorphism core `presheafDualUnitIso_naturality`
(DualInverse.lean:317, sorry-free) must be promoted to the immersion `j`.

## Target 3 ‚Äî `trivialisation_restrict_compat` (telescope, L2335) ‚Äî BLOCKED
Gated on all five squares (S2/S3/S4b done; S4a OPEN; S4c) + chart-chase telescope assembly. Not
attempted; strictly downstream of S4a. In-file docs L2294-2334 current.

## Review subagents
- **lean-auditor** `iter084`: 0 critical / 0 major / **3 minor** ‚Äî `task_results/lean-auditor-iter084.md`.
  Proof genuine (real steps, no sorry/circularity); `dual_scp_assemble` sound + used once (L1489);
  S3 sorry-free; the 2 remaining sorries genuine + correctly scaffolded. Minors: (1) stale `.lean`
  comment L1169 ("see the `dual_restrict_iso_restrict_compat` sorry below" ‚Äî S3 now closed);
  (2) dead private helper `iso_symm_trans_cancel` L1149 (0 call sites, superseded by `iso_symm_fold_cancel`);
  (3) dead private helper `sheafificationCompPullback_restrict` L1101 (0 call sites, superseded by L1 folded route).
- **lean-vs-blueprint-checker** `tensorobjinverse-iter084`: **PASS, 0 must-fix** / 1 major / 1 minor ‚Äî
  `task_results/lean-vs-blueprint-checker-tensorobjinverse-iter084.md`. L1 statement term-for-term match;
  proof route faithful (dual-B1 ‚Üí B2 RFIP-cancel ‚Üí bridge (b) ‚Üí bridge (c) ‚Üí c.1 ‚Üí `.hom`-flatten +
  `dual_scp_assemble`); axiom-clean verified; sorry count = exactly 2. Major (carried, non-blocking):
  L1 `\uses`/prose still cite chart B2 `lem:restrictfunctorisopullback_comp_compat` but the proof uses
  the un-blueprinted general `restrictFunctorIsoPullback_comp_general(_hom)`. Minor: `dual_scp_assemble`
  un-blueprinted.

## Blueprint markers updated (manual)
- `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex` `lem:dual_restrict_iso_comp` proof block (~L8436): added `\leanok`
  (manual override ‚Äî `sync_leanok` iter-084 reported added 0/removed 0 but the chapter convention uses
  proof-block `\leanok`, 126 instances, and this PUBLIC proof is verifiably closed: green build re-run
  this review + lvb axiom-clean kernel-only). Justification: positive sync miss on a complete proof.
- `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex` `lem:dual_restrict_iso_pullbackcongr` proof block (~L8490): added
  `\leanok` (private brick, proven iter-083, build green, axiom-clean) ‚Äî same sync miss.
- `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex` `lem:dual_restrict_iso_restrict_compat` (S3) proof block (~L8527):
  added `\leanok` (private; now fully sorry-free riding the closed L1 + L2; lvb verified axiom-clean).
- `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex` `lem:dual_restrict_iso_comp` `% NOTE` (~L8411): refreshed to record
  L1 CLOSED; B2-coverage debt + un-blueprinted `dual_scp_assemble` are the only remaining L1 gaps.

NOTE on sync: `sync_leanok-state.json` = iter 84, added 0/removed 0, chapters_touched []. The three
proof-block `\leanok`s above were genuine sync misses (likely private-decl name resolution for S3/L2;
the L1 miss is the clearer case ‚Äî public + verified). Surfaced here per the override-justification rule.

## Key findings / patterns
- **WINNING IDIOM (folded Scheme-dual cocycle across the `SheafOfModules ‚â´` seam):** `apply Iso.ext`
  ‚Üí syntactic `.hom` flatten (`Iso.trans_hom`+`Functor.mapIso_hom`+`Functor.map_comp`+`Iso.symm_hom`+
  `Iso.app_hom`; `Category.assoc` fires at `.hom` level, `Œ∏_g`/`sliceDualTransport` stay folded) ‚Üí
  discharge a pure-abstract single-`[Category C]` assembly lemma by `exact` (no whnf, beats the
  monolithic Scheme-typed assembly that whnf-bombs).
- **NEVER stay iso-level**: `rw [hb]`/`simp [Iso.trans_assoc]` MISS the seam; `erw [hb]` fires but the
  bracket then resists iso-level reassoc.
- Mid-pair cancellation under `sheafify`: keyed `rw [Iso.hom_inv_id_app]` MISSES the presheaf-over-Z
  composite-functor seam ‚Üí use term-mode `congrArg`.

## Recommendations for next session
See `recommendations.md`. Headline: S4a `dual_unit_iso_restrict_compat` is the next frontier ‚Äî it is a
NEW separable construction (Œ∏-at-unit bridge), so **effort-break it into the Œ∏-unit identification
lemma FIRST**, do not re-grind monolithically. Telescope stays gated on S4a.
